title: US EPA to Rule on Backlog of Small Refinery Biofuel Exemption Requests, Zeldin Says
content:
On May 15, 2025, the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) submitted two major Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS) actions to the White House Office of Management and Budget for review: the proposed Renewable Volume Obligations (RVOs) for 2026 and beyond, and a final rule to partially waive the 2024 cellulosic biofuel mandate.
Separately, EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin stated the agency will move quickly to rule on 161 pending small refinery exemption applications, which had been left undecided under the Biden administration. These decisions are expected to significantly affect the multi-billion-dollar RIN (Renewable Identification Number) credit market and could influence biofuel blending compliance costs for refiners. The agency is also expected to meet statutory rulemaking deadlines for future RVOs and will open a public comment period in the coming months. Discussions around E15 fuel sales continue, with Administrator Zeldin indicating that congressional action would provide the most durable solution for year-round availability. These developments come as part of the Trump administrationâ€™s broader energy policy and fiscal priorities for FY2026. View here for more details: https://www.appropriations.senate.gov/hearings/a-review-of-the-presidents-fiscal-year-2026-budget-request-for-the-environmental-protection-agency
author: Analysts Account
